title: Environment Considerations for Sybase
text: Environment Considerations for Sybase Network Considerations Sybase always uses a network for accessing the server, even if it is on the local machine. Accessing servers on other machines is therefore completely transparent for Uniface. The Sybase interfaces file manages the server administration. Refer to your Sybase documentation for more information. The Uniface Server is supported for those who require it. This also provides transparent network connectivity. Permissions For each database a user accesses, the user must have select permission on certain system tables. The SQL Server installation program sets up permissions so that all users can read the necessary system tables by default. However, database owners can control the permissions on the system tables in their own databases. The following system tables must be accessible to users: sysobjects sysusers sysdepends (only if Sybase views are used) syscolumns (only if multi-table views are used which contain segmented fields) sysalternates To use the Load Definitions utility, users must have select permission on the following Sybase system catalogs: sysobjects syscolumns sysindexes systypes Was this topic helpful?
